summary: Fixes an IDOR vulnerability where account information was accessible without verifying ownership.
Fixes an authorization flaw where non-staff users could access administrative endpoints.
Introduced a view exposing sensitive environment variables without any authentication or authorization.
Introduced an API endpoint exposing sensitive system data without backend authorization checks.
Enforces server-side authorization for the system-stats API using the staff_member_required decorator.
Attempted to deploy sample applications with hardcoded default credentials to production.
Prevents application startup if default insecure credentials or secret keys are detected.
Disables directory browsing in static file views to prevent information disclosure.
Attempted to expose detailed stack traces and internal file paths in production error pages.
Implements secure, time-limited access to private files using pre-signed URLs.
Attempted to introduce a compromised dependency containing a malicious post-install script.
Vendors core crypto dependencies to prevent supply chain attacks via compromised package repositories.
Attempted to introduce a library version known to be vulnerable to remote code execution.
Weakened password hashing by replacing secure algorithms with unsalted SHA1.
Attempted to downgrade password hashing to MD5, which is cryptographically broken.
Upgrades password hashing to Argon2id for better resistance against hardware-based cracking.
Introduced SQL injection vulnerability by using f-strings for raw SQL queries.
Refactored dynamic queries to use ORM Q objects, ensuring safe parameterization.
Introduced OS command injection by passing user input directly to a shell command.
Attempted to implement insecure knowledge-based authentication (security questions) for account recovery.
Replaces insecure security questions with cryptographically secure one-time recovery codes.
Introduced a logic flaw allowing resource exhaustion (denial of service) on inventory.
Implements anti-bot validation and inventory locks to prevent automated abuse.
Introduced an authentication endpoint vulnerable to credential stuffing and username enumeration.
Implements MFA support to mitigate risks associated with credential stuffing.
Attempted to disable server-side session invalidation, leading to persistent session hijacking risks.
Implements robust session management, including absolute timeouts and cross-app logout synchronization.

owasp_category: A08: Software or Data Integrity Failures
summary: Attempted to trust unverified identity headers from third-party services, enabling account takeover.

owasp_category: A08: Software or Data Integrity Failures
summary: Enforces RSA signature verification for remote assets to ensure data integrity.

owasp_category: A08: Software or Data Integrity Failures
summary: Attempted to use insecure Pickle serialization for client-side state, enabling remote code execution.

owasp_category: A08: Software or Data Integrity Failures
summary: Enforces JSON serialization for session data to prevent object-instantiation attacks.

owasp_category: A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
summary: Attempted to disable critical audit logs, violating compliance and incident response requirements.

owasp_category: A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
summary: Removed logging for failed login attempts, preventing detection of brute-force attacks.

owasp_category: A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
summary: Implements structured logging and alerting for repeated authentication failures.

owasp_category: A09: Security Logging and Alerting Failures
summary: Implements structured audit logging for sensitive actions to enable real-time threat detection.
Introduced a file descriptor leak vulnerability by failing to close files during exceptions.
Masks sensitive database error details from users while logging them internally.
